coverage: Solar value chain · greenfield-IRR model config (structural inputs)

assumptions: Pre-tax, unlevered project IRR · level annual EBITDA as the cash proxy · single upfront CapEx.
Per Watt of annual output capacity · EBITDA/W = price/W × margin × utilisation.
Manufacturing life ~10–15 yrs · generation 25-yr PPA · steady-state utilisation (India module lines run ~40–50%).
Prices/tariff derived from the latest price-stack & tenders data; CapEx & DCR premium maintained. Ignores leverage, tax, ramp & working capital — a relative value-capture read, not a bankable return.

rows.note: Global commodity (no India base). ASP derived from the live poly price (~2.6 g/W); prices at/below cash cost -> capital never recovered.
Global (China ~97%). ASP derived from the live wafer price (~7.5 W/piece); duopoly ran below cash cost -> negative EBITDA, no positive IRR.
TOPCon line ~Rs 595 cr/GW. India realised ASP = live China cell spot x ~2.0 DCR scarcity premium; margin = pure-cell (Premier 27 · Websol 44).
Integrated cell+module line (~Rs 700 cr/GW). ASP = live China module spot x ~1.0 (India realised); pure assembly is capex-light but competed away at ~40-45% utilisation.
Rs 4.2 cr/MW. Annual revenue/W = latest awarded solar tariff x 24% CUF x 8,760 h. 25-yr PPA; ~9-11% unlevered project-IRR benchmark.

notes: This snapshot holds only the STRUCTURAL inputs (CapEx intensity, utilisation, asset life, the India price premium and the maintained pure-stage margin). The volatile inputs — the representative price/W and the generation tariff — are DERIVED at render time from the freshest committed data (the monthly price stack and the tenders tariff feed), so the IRR always uses the latest source without re-hand-editing.

The IRR itself (pre-tax, unlevered project IRR = solve CapEx = Σ EBITDA/(1+r)ᵗ over the asset life) is computed at render time and is Munshot analysis. CapEx & prices are sourced FACT; loss-making stages (poly, wafer) return no positive IRR.
